LAW AND MOTION 302, PETITIONER MERCURY INSURANCE COMPANY'S PETITION TO ALLOW REVIEW OF UNREDACTED MEDICAL RECORDS OF UNINSURED MOTORIST CLAIMANT UTKU KANDEMIR BY COURT OR THIRD PARTY WITH RESPECT OT WHETHER THEY ARE RELEVANT AND SHOULD BE PROVIDED WAS HEARD ON AND TAKEN UNDER SUBMISSION ON MARCH 3, 2021 BY THE HON. ETHAN P. SCHULMAN AND IS NOW RULED ON AS FOLLOWS: THE REPORT AND RECOMMENDATION OF THE JUDGE PRO TEM IS ADOPTED IN PART, AS FOLLOWS: PURUSANT TO CODE OF CIVIL PROCEDURE SECTION 639 AND THE PARTIES' AGREEMENT, THE COURT APPOINTS HON. BONNIE SABRAW (RET.) (ADR SERVICES) AS THE DISCOVERY REFEREE FOR THE IN CAMERA REVIEW OF RESPONDENT UTKU KANDEMIR'S MEDICAL AND BILLING RECORDS FROM UCSF, THE REFEREE'S FEES SHALL BE SPLIT 50%/50% BY THE PARTIES; SUBJECT TO THE FEE SCHEDULE OF ADR SERVICES. RESPONDENT'S REQUEST FOR SANCTIONS IS DENIED. (SEE ORDER FILED THIS DATE FOR THE FULL DETAILS OF THE COURT'S RULING)

Petitioner Mercury Insurance Company'S Petition To Allow Review Of Unredacted Medical Records Of Uninsured Motorist Claimant Utku Kandemir By Court Or Third Party With Respect Ot Whether They Are Relevant And Should Be Provided
Off Calendar Mar-04-2021 Text Ruling
MINI MINUTES:

APPEARANCES (VIA ZOOM VIDEOCONFERENCE):
Atty. STEPHEN R. BARRY, Esq. of Cesari, Werner and Moriarty (Tel: 650-991-5126) for petitioner Mercury Insurance Company;
Atty. ROSS J. PSYHOGIOS, Esq. of Jones Clifford, LLP (Tel: 415-431-5310) for respondent Utku Kandemir.

Hearing Re: Petitioner Mercury Insurance Company's Petition To Allow Review Of Unredacted Medical Records Of Uninsured Motorist Claimant Utku Kandemir By Court Or Third Party With Respect Ot Whether They Are Relevant And Should Be Provided.

Matter argued and submitted. The Court takes the matter under submission.

Judge: Ethan P. Schulman; Clerk: Sean Kane; Court Reporter: Maria Torreano, CSR No. 8600, Email: maria.torreano@gmail.com (302/EPS)

Notice Of Hearing Re: Petition To Allow Review Of Unredacted Medical Records Of Uninsured Motorist Claimant Utku Kandemir By Court Or Third Party With Respect Ot Whether They Are Relevant And Should Be Provided
Off Calendar Feb-04-2021 Text Ruling
MINI MINUTES:

Hearing Re: Petitioner Mercury Insurance Company's Petition To Allow Review Of Unredacted Medical Records Of Uninsured Motorist Claimant Utku Kandemir By Court Or Third Party With Respect To Whether They Are Relevant And Should Be Provided.

No appearances made in this matter. The Court adopts its tentative ruling. Matter is transferred to be heard at 9:00 a.m. on the discovery calendar (Room 301) on the same date.

Judge: Ethan P. Schulman; Clerk: Sean Kane; Not Reported (302/EPS)

Petitioner Mercury Insurance Company'S Petition To Allow Review Of Unredacted Medical Records Of Uninsured Motorist Claimant Utku Kandemir By Court Or Third Party With Respect Ot Whether They Are Relevant And Should Be Provided
Off Calendar Feb-04-2021 Continued To Mar-03-21 At 09:30 A.M. In Dept 302
MINI MINUTES:
APPEARANCES VIA ZOOM VIDEOCONFERENCE:
Stephen Barry for Petitioner/Moving Party, Mercury Insurance Company.
Ross Psyhogios (415-431-5310) of Jones Clifford, LLP for Respondent, Dr. Kandemir.

Respondent's Counsel does not stipulate to the matter being heard by the Judge Pro Tem.

ARGUED. The Court having reviewed the pleadings and having heard oral argument rules as follows:

Petitioner Mercury Insurance Company'S Petition To Allow Review Of Unredacted Medical Records Of Uninsured Motorist Claimant Utku Kandemir By Court Or Third Party With Respect Ot Whether They Are Relevant And Should Be Provided:
The matter is continued to March 3, 2021 at 9:30 am in Department 302, Law and Motion Department.
Judge Pro Tem to prepare report and recommendation.

Judge Pro Tem: David McDonald, Clerk: S. Le, Not reported. =(302/JPT)
